title: Scientists Use Light to Flip a Magnet
summary: Scientists from two universities in Switzerland have found a new way to change a magnet's direction using only a laser light. Magnets work because tiny parts inside them, called spins, all point the same way, like a team working together. Usually, to flip a magnet, you need to heat it up to make the spins move around and then cool it down so they point a new way. But these scientists did it with light without heating, which is a big breakthrough. They used a special material made of thin layers twisted slightly. This lets them control the magnet with light, which could help make faster and smaller electronics in the future. The magnet is very small, only a few micrometers wide, and they checked it flipped by shining another light on it and seeing how it reflected.

background_read: Magnets are common in everyday life, like in fridge doors or compasses. They work because tiny particles inside them align in one direction. Scientists study magnets to improve technology, such as making computers faster. In this article, researchers found a new way to change a magnet's direction using light instead of heat, which could lead to new electronic devices.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists from the University of Basel and ETH Zurich
WHAT: They used a laser to flip a magnet's direction without heating
WHERE: In Switzerland at research labs
WHY: To help design better electronics in the future

perspectives.description: This discovery shows a new way to control magnets with light, which could advance technology.
